title: Volven Roxe

'''Volven Roxe''', also known as "'''the wolfman'''," was a [[Shistavanen/Legends|Shistavanen]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who operated as a [[spice lord]] and [[Crime lord/Legends|crime kingpin]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. He was the owner of several [[Nightclub/Legends|nightclubs]] in Coruscant's [[Uscru Entertainment District/Legends|Uscru Entertainment District]], one of which was the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]]. Roxe was a rival of the fellow club owners [[Beuga Odell]] and [[Quetemoor the Sub-Elder]] and an addict of the drug [[Drovian zwil]]. 

==Biography==
[[File:Outlander_Club.png|thumb|350px|right|Volven Roxe owned the Outlander Club (pictured).]]
Volven Roxe, also referred to as "the wolfman," was a [[Shistavanen/Legends|Shistavanen]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime kingpin]] local to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where he was the world's top [[spice lord]]. He was the owner of the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]],<ref name="CatCW" /> a [[Nightclub/Legends|nightclub]] and [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] hall located in the [[Uscru Entertainment District/Legends|Uscru Entertainment District]] of Coruscant.<ref name="OutlanderCSWE">{{CSWECite|II|431|Outlander Club}}</ref> Though the Outlander's proprietors were a secretive band of [[Columi/Legends|Columi]]<ref name="CatCW" /> and the club was managed by the dangerous [[Baath brothers/Legends|Baath brothers]],<ref name="BaathCSWE">{{CSWECite|I|54|Baath Brothers}}</ref> Roxe was the true owner of the club. Roxe also owned several other nightclubs near the Outlander, through which he competed for control of the entertainment district with his rivals: club owners [[Beuga Odell]] and [[Quetemoor the Sub-Elder]], and [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant's undercity]] [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] bosses.<ref name="CatCW" />

Similarly to his rivals, Roxe made most of the profit from the Outlander Club though illegal substances sold by gorgeous clubgoers on the Shistavanen's [[Currency/Legends|payroll]]. Unlike other crime lords, however, he kept his reach restricted to import and sale of the drugs [[Andris/Legends|andris]], [[Ryll/Legends|ryll]], [[Glitterstim/Legends|glitterstim]], and [[lumni-spice]]. At some point, Roxe placed a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on a woman known superstitiously to him as "the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]." The woman was, in actuality, former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Lexia Ginorra]], who led a [[Blood/Legends|bloody]] crusade against crime. At another point, Roxe had hired the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Anniha Nega]],<ref name="CatCW" /> who was active in the last [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="AnnihaCSWE">{{CSWECite|II|368|Nega, Anniha}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Roxe, a preening, narcissistic, and [[Speciesism|xenophobic]] man, exhibited [[Color/Legends|red]] and black<ref name="CatCW" /> striped<ref name="SWI752">{{InsiderCite|75|Who's Who in the Outlander Club: It's Ladies' Night}}</ref> [[Hair/Legends|hair]], a trait unusual for a member of the Shistavanen [[Species/Legends|species]]. Should a group have caused a scene at the Outlander, they could have possibly found themselves dragged to Roxe to receive personal chastisement.<ref name="CatCW" /> 

The man's owning of several clubs made him a target for rival gangsters who occasionally sought to put him in his place. Beuga Odell would have dearly loved to destroy Roxe's criminal organization but refrained from doing so as to not trigger a gang war. Roxe rarely appeared in public and made a point to not try out the drugs he trafficked, though he was addicted to a drug unrelated to his work, [[Drovian zwil]].<ref name="CatCW" />

==Skills and abilities==
The Shistavanen could speak in the [[Shistavanen (language)|Shistavanen]], [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]], and [[Mugaar (language)|Mugaar]] languages. He could also read and write in Shistavanen. The crime kingpin was proficient in the usage of [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]] and simple [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] and was knowledgeable on Coruscant and business. Skills possessed by Roxe included bluffing, escape artistry, gathering information, appraisal, hiding, and sleight of hand. A [[Scoundrel/Legends|scoundrel]], Roxe had improved initiative, mobility and dodging ability, and alertness.<ref name="CatCW" />

==Equipment==
Roxe used a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]], a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], and an engraved [[zwil]] container.<ref name="CatCW" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:CoruscantandtheCoreWorlds.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Volven Roxe was introduced in ''Coruscant and the Core Worlds''.]]
Volven Roxe was first mentioned in ''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'', a [[2003]] sourcebook for the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' published by [[Wizards of the Coast]]. In the adventure scenario "Troglodyte Terror," the heroes playing trace the missing [[Veritas Press]] employee [[Elbee Bribb]] to Roxe's crime den in the worst part of the Coruscant undercity. If a player is discreet, they may learn that Bribb had been taken, and that he had sold the Shistavanen an unpublished exposé of Coruscant's spice trade. Players may be able to get Roxe to aide them in wiping out Bribb's kidnappers, the mutated subterranean [[Scavenger/Legends|scavengers]] known as [[Cthon/Legends|Cthons]], if they convince him that they are a threat to his turf.<ref name="CatCW" />

When writing for the character [[Magaloof/Legends|Magaloof]] through the [[StarWars.com]] [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] feature [[What's The Story?]], author [[Gregory Walker]] originally wrote that Magaloof aspired to join Roxe, though this was changed to the criminal organization [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] in the editing process.<ref name="NarbFlick">{{Blog|narbflick|7|Magaloof: The Story Behind the Story}}</ref>
